nodes.label: Gradient-flow dynamics instrumentation
nodes.rationale: Attach a lightweight per-layer gradient logger to a training run to study when and why training degrades, capturing vanishing/exploding/collapse anomalies.

nodes.label: Anomaly-to-performance-drop lead time
nodes.rationale: Measure the lead time between early per-layer gradient-flow anomalies and an observable drop in validation performance, to prove gradient signals warn ahead of failure.

nodes.label: grader-drift-pipeline (gradescope)
nodes.rationale: Phases 0-3 of a conveyor-belt grading CNN failure-early-warning plan; synthetic-only validation because no real production frames or QC labels exist.

nodes.label: Phase 0 synthetic slow-drift generator
nodes.rationale: drift.py generates lighting/blur/dust/colortemp corruption ramps with a ground-truth manifest.csv timeline; seed-reproducible, byte-identical for the same seed.

nodes.label: WindowDetector unified alarm interface
nodes.rationale: Rolling-window KS + CUSUM alarm framework against a clean deployment-start reference, shared by Phase 1 windowed detectors and Phase 2 batch detectors.

nodes.label: Windowed GradNorm production detector
nodes.rationale: Windowed GradNorm (uniform-KL, last layer, KS+CUSUM) chosen as production detector: earliest or tied on all 4 ramps, 0 clean false alarms, cheapest closed-form gradient signal (no autograd).

nodes.label: Phase 3 supervised fragility gate
nodes.rationale: gate.py fine-tunes a retrained candidate on the weekly labeled QC batch (backbone frozen) and applies a cold-start relative dual rule: BLOCK if GSNR < 0.8x incumbent or QC accuracy < incumbent - 0.02.

nodes.label: Stand-in conveyor CNN
nodes.rationale: standin.py: since no production model exists, a small CNN trained on procedural 'fruit on conveyor' frames (3 blemish-driven grades, clean accuracy 0.945) stands in.

nodes.label: GradNorm-not-GSNR detection ruling
nodes.rationale: User ruling 2026-07-03: the unlabeled detection path (Phases 1-2) uses GradNorm, not GSNR. GSNR is used only in the supervised Phase 3 gate; both come from the same closed-form per-sample gradients and the metric is config-switchable.

nodes.label: Batch-level detector NO-GO decision
nodes.rationale: 2026-07-04 go/no-go: NO-GO for the batch-level layer as primary detector. Batch entropy-GradNorm gains 1 step on lighting but loses 7-10 steps on colortemp (the slow-drift target regime); batch code stays but windowed GradNorm is primary.

nodes.label: cnn-grader-doctor MCP server
nodes.rationale: Analysis-only MCP server that diagnoses trained CNN grader models (confusion matrices, grade distributions, image quality); kept thin and separate from the drift pipeline and gradscope-mcp.

nodes.label: Cold-start relative dual-metric gate rule
nodes.rationale: GSNR alone is insufficient: undertrained candidate shows higher GSNR and would pass a GSNR-only gate, so the QC-accuracy leg is necessary. No absolute threshold until >=5 cycles of history.

nodes.label: GSNR gate metric (generalization-gap signal)
nodes.rationale: Last-layer per-sample gradient GSNR separates the overfit candidate (-32% vs incumbent) even when its training loss on the memorized set is near zero; GSNR tracks the generalization gap.

nodes.label: Phase 2 config ablations
nodes.rationale: Raw entropy-gradient mean is heavy-tailed on clean data causing false alarms; temperature T=2 inside the gradient or median aggregation fixes it at no detection-speed cost. Batch 256 confirmed; last layer beats last block.

nodes.label: gradscope-mcp live monitoring server
nodes.rationale: FastMCP stdio server surfacing per-layer GSNR, layer health states, training status and lead times by reading results/live_state.json (overwritten each epoch) plus experiment CSVs, keeping an in-memory history.

nodes.label: Layer health state classification
nodes.rationale: States HEALTHY/STAGNANT/NOISY/DEAD from per-layer GSNR thresholds (tau_h=0.1, 1e-8, 1e-10), using the same thresholds as logger/layer_classifier.py.

nodes.label: GSNR signal and lead-time estimation
nodes.rationale: GSNR signal fires once any layer is NOISY or DEAD; failure is a sustained >5% relative drop in validation accuracy over 3 epochs; lead time = failure epoch - signal epoch (positive = advance warning).
